event: Transporting a child's coffin by boat
symbols: small coffin — the death of a child, the particular anguish of losing the young
boat crossing water — the passage between life and death, the crossing from the world of the living to rest
black mourning garments — grief, loss, and the solemn observance of death
calm still water — the quietude of sorrow, the hush that death imposes on the living
description: A wooden rowing boat carries a small group of mourners across a calm, expansive body of water under a pale overcast sky. At the center sits a woman in full black mourning dress with a white collar, holding what appears to be a small wrapped bundle. Beside her, a young boy in dark clothing looks ahead somberly. A small green coffin lid is visible in the boat. Two men in white shirts row at the bow and stern. A young girl in a dark dress sits near the stern. Distant islands and other boats are faintly visible on the horizon.
